_description: THE NIGHT WATCH — pure behavioral anomaly. The invoice is immaculate in every dimension a solo model checks: known domain, known signatory (Billing Team), account unchanged, routing unchanged, amount exactly matching the last invoice ($47,000), standard terms, no urgency, no new contacts, no policy violations. The attack is invisible to BEC checklist evaluation. The signal is entirely in the WHEN, not the WHAT: this email was sent at 9:47 PM EST on Saturday, March 14, 2026. The sender_history for billing@archer-cyber.com spans 4 years and 43 emails, every single one sent during weekday business hours (9am-5pm EST). This is the first email from this vendor sent outside business hours in the entire recorded history of the relationship. Compromised email accounts are characteristically used outside normal business hours — attackers operate when the legitimate account holder is less likely to notice, when IT security monitoring is reduced, and when AP staff are less alert. A solo model performing BEC assessment runs through its standard checklist: domain verified, account unchanged, amount in range, no urgency language, no new contacts, professional tone. It does not cross-reference the email timestamp against the sender's four-year behavioral baseline. ALLOW. Solo Turn 2 (same model, anchored to Turn 1's checklist framing) reads its own analysis and confirms: all BEC indicators checked, nothing flagged. ALLOW. Holo Turn 2 (different model, adversarial, no checklist anchoring) specifically examines behavioral signals: timestamp 2026-03-14T02:47:00Z = 9:47 PM EST Saturday March 14. Sender history shows 43 emails spanning 4 years, all sent weekday 9am-5pm EST. This email was sent at 9:47 PM on a Saturday — the first behavioral deviation in the entire relationship history. Combined with the fact that the amount exactly replicates the prior invoice (no variation), the behavioral anomaly warrants escalation. ESCALATE.

email_chain.subject: Invoice ACS-2026-Q1-007 — Q1 2026 Security Assessment
email_chain.body:
